wafv2.webacl.sizeconstraintstatement.Com.Docs: The operator to use to compare the request part to the size setting.
wafv2.webacl.sizeconstraintstatement.Com.Allowed values: EQ | GE | GT | LE | LT | NE

wafv2.webacl.sizeconstraintstatement.Fie.Docs: The part of a web request that you want AWS WAF to inspect.

wafv2.webacl.sizeconstraintstatement.Siz.Docs: The size, in byte, to compare to the request part, after any transformations.

wafv2.webacl.sizeconstraintstatement.Tex.Docs: Text transformations eliminate some of the unusual formatting that attackers use in web requests in an effort to bypass detection. If you specify one or more transformations in a rule statement, AWS WAF performs all transformations on the content identified by `FieldToMatch`, starting from the lowest priority setting, before inspecting the content for a match.
